p.m: \displaystyle{\begin{aligned} 52 \amp = 4 -3(C-8) \amp 52 \amp = 4 -3(C-8) \\ 52 \amp = 4 +(-3) \cdot (C+(-8)) \amp 52\mathbf{{}-4} \amp = 4 -3(C-8)\mathbf{{}-4} \\ 52 \amp = 4 +(-3) \cdot C+(-3) \cdot (-8) \amp 48 \amp = -3(C-8) \\ 52 \amp = 4 -3C +24 \amp \frac{48}{-3} \amp = \frac{-3(C-8)}{-3} \\ 52 \amp = -3C +(4+24) \amp -16 \amp = C-8 \\ 52 \amp = -3C +28 \amp -16\mathbf{{}+8} \amp = C-8\mathbf{{}+8} \\ 52\mathbf{{}-28} \amp = -3C +28\mathbf{{}-28} \amp -8 \amp = C \\ 24 \amp = -3C \amp C \amp = -8 \\ \frac{24}{-3} \amp = \frac{-3C}{-3} \\ -8 \amp = C\\ C \amp = -8 \end{aligned} }

solution.p: The solution to this equation is -8. To stress that this is a value assigned to C, some report C = -8. We can also say that the solution set is \{-8\}, or that C\in\left\{-8\right\}. If we substitute -8 in for C in the original equation 52=4-3\mathopen{}\left(C-8\right), the equation will be true. Please check this on your own; it is an important habit.
